name: senet-board-game-egypt
description: Egyptians played Senet, a race board game of squares thought to symbolize the soul's journey to the afterlife.

name: royal-game-of-ur
description: A two-player race game played in Mesopotamia, its inlaid boards were unearthed in the royal tombs of Ur.

name: mehen-snake-game
description: Ancient Egyptians played Mehen on a spiral board shaped like a coiled snake, using marble pieces and lions.

name: minoan-bull-leaping
description: Athletes on Bronze Age Crete vaulted over charging bulls, a ritual sport depicted in Minoan palace frescoes.

name: egyptian-wrestling-beni-hasan
description: Tomb walls at Beni Hasan show hundreds of wrestling holds, the earliest detailed record of the sport.

name: first-ancient-olympics
description: Greeks held the first recorded Olympic Games at Olympia, honoring Zeus with footraces and athletic contests.

name: go-game-china
description: The strategy board game Go, played with black and white stones on a grid, was established in ancient China.

name: pankration-added-olympics
description: Greeks introduced pankration, a brutal mix of boxing and wrestling with almost no rules, to the Olympic Games.

name: pythian-games-founded
description: Held at Delphi to honor Apollo, the Pythian Games joined the Olympics among Greece's great athletic festivals.

name: knucklebones-astragaloi
description: Greeks and Romans played knucklebones, tossing small animal ankle bones in a popular game of skill and chance.

name: mesoamerican-ballgame-rise
description: Olmec peoples played a ritual ball game with a heavy rubber ball, a tradition spanning Mesoamerican cultures.

name: petteia-greek-board-game
description: Greeks played petteia, a strategy game of capturing pieces on a board, an ancestor of later war games.

name: cuju-football-china
description: Han dynasty China played cuju, a kicking ball game using a leather ball and a goal, an ancient form of football.

name: circus-maximus-races
description: Rome's Circus Maximus hosted thundering chariot races before crowds of tens of thousands of spectators.

name: first-roman-gladiator-games
description: Romans staged their first gladiatorial combat as a funeral tribute, beginning centuries of arena spectacle.

name: ludus-latrunculorum
description: Romans played latrunculi, a two-player strategy board game of military maneuver popular across the empire.

name: tabula-roman-backgammon
description: Romans favored tabula, a dice-and-counters race game on a board that became the ancestor of backgammon.

name: patolli-aztec-game
description: Mesoamericans gambled on patolli, a race game on a cross-shaped board played with marked beans as dice.

name: chess-emerges-india
description: The game chaturanga developed in India, evolving into chess with pieces representing the parts of an army.

name: playing-cards-tang-china
description: China developed the earliest playing cards during the Tang dynasty, spreading the games westward over centuries.

name: hnefatafl-viking-game
description: Norse peoples played hnefatafl, an asymmetric board game where a king sought escape from surrounding attackers.

name: shogi-japan
description: Japan developed shogi, a chess-like game in which captured pieces can be returned to the board to fight again.

name: xiangqi-chinese-chess
description: Xiangqi, Chinese chess played with a river dividing the board, took its classic form during the Song dynasty.

name: byzantine-hippodrome-races
description: Constantinople's Hippodrome hosted fierce chariot races whose fan factions even sparked deadly city riots.

name: real-tennis-medieval
description: French monks and nobles played jeu de paume, striking a ball by hand, the ancestor of modern racket tennis.

name: mob-football-england
description: Medieval English villages played chaotic mob football, with whole crowds chasing a ball between settlements.

name: scotland-bans-golf-1457
description: Scotland's parliament banned golf and football so men would practice archery for national defense instead.

name: medieval-jousting-tournaments
description: European knights competed in jousting tournaments, charging on horseback with lances to unseat their rivals.

name: tarot-cards-italy
description: Italian courts created tarot decks with trump cards for trick-taking games long before they were used to divine.

name: modern-chess-queen-rules
description: European players gave the queen and bishop sweeping moves, creating the faster game of chess played today.

name: mary-queen-scots-billiards
description: Billiards moved from a lawn game to indoor tables in Europe, becoming a fashionable pastime of the nobility.

name: first-recorded-cricket
description: England produced the earliest clear records of cricket, a bat-and-ball game that grew from village contests.

name: ascot-racecourse-founded
description: Queen Anne founded Ascot racecourse near Windsor, beginning one of horse racing's most prestigious meetings.

name: first-boxing-rules-broughton
description: English champion Jack Broughton drew up boxing's first rules, banning hitting a downed opponent in the ring.

name: jockey-club-founded
description: Britain's Jockey Club formed to govern horse racing, standardizing rules for the rapidly growing sport.

name: marylebone-cricket-club
description: The Marylebone Cricket Club formed at Lord's in London, becoming the long-time guardian of cricket's laws.

name: first-epsom-derby
description: England ran the first Epsom Derby, a horse race for three-year-olds that became a model for derbies worldwide.

name: snakes-and-ladders-india
description: India played a moral board game of virtues and vices that later spread worldwide as Snakes and Ladders.

name: first-jigsaw-puzzle
description: Mapmaker John Spilsbury mounted a map on wood and cut it into pieces, inventing the educational jigsaw puzzle.

name: checkers-draughts-standardized
description: English writers codified the rules of draughts, the board game of jumping pieces known in America as checkers.

name: first-baseball-knickerbocker
description: New York's Knickerbocker club wrote rules for baseball, shaping the diamond, bases, and play of the game.

name: first-modern-rugby
description: Pupils at Rugby School in England wrote down rules for their handling form of football, founding the sport.

name: queensberry-boxing-rules
description: The Marquess of Queensberry rules introduced gloves and timed rounds, shaping the modern sport of boxing.

name: fa-football-rules-1863
description: England's Football Association set unified rules for soccer, splitting it from the handling game of rugby.

name: first-kentucky-derby
description: The first Kentucky Derby was run at Churchill Downs in Louisville, launching a famous American horse race.

name: first-wimbledon-championship
description: The All England Club held the first Wimbledon tennis tournament, the oldest of the game's major championships.

name: first-test-cricket-match
description: Australia and England met in the first international Test cricket match, played over several days in Melbourne.

name: first-stanley-cup
description: Canada first awarded the Stanley Cup to its top ice hockey team, beginning the sport's most coveted trophy.

name: basketball-invented-naismith
description: James Naismith hung peach baskets in a Massachusetts gym and devised basketball as an indoor winter sport.

name: volleyball-invented
description: William Morgan created volleyball in Massachusetts as a gentler indoor game blending tennis and handball.

name: ioc-founded-coubertin
description: Pierre de Coubertin founded the International Olympic Committee in Paris to revive the ancient Olympic Games.

name: first-boston-marathon
description: Runners contested the first Boston Marathon, which became the world's oldest annual marathon footrace.

name: first-tour-de-france
description: Cyclists rode the first Tour de France, a grueling multi-stage race around the country to sell newspapers.

name: fifa-founded
description: Seven nations founded FIFA in Paris to govern international soccer and organize matches between countries.

name: first-world-series
description: Baseball's American and National Leagues met in the first World Series, won by the Boston Americans.

name: first-indianapolis-500
description: Cars raced 500 miles at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway in the first running of the famous American race.

name: monopoly-board-game
description: Parker Brothers launched Monopoly, the property-trading board game that became one of the best-selling ever.

name: first-world-cup-1930
description: Uruguay hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup, launching soccer's premier international tournament.

name: babe-ruth-60-home-runs
description: Babe Ruth hit 60 home runs in a single season for the Yankees, a baseball record that stood for decades.

name: crossword-puzzle-published
description: A New York newspaper printed the first crossword puzzle, sparking a craze for the word-grid game.

name: scrabble-invented
description: Alfred Butts designed the tile-based word game later sold as Scrabble, scoring points for words on a grid.

name: jesse-owens-four-golds
description: American sprinter Jesse Owens won four gold medals at the Berlin Olympics, defying Nazi racial propaganda.

name: jackie-robinson-mlb-debut
description: Jackie Robinson debuted for the Brooklyn Dodgers, breaking Major League Baseball's racial color line.

name: bannister-four-minute-mile
description: Roger Bannister ran a mile in under four minutes in Oxford, breaking a barrier long thought impossible.

name: first-summer-paralympics
description: Rome hosted the first Paralympic Games for athletes with disabilities, held just after the Summer Olympics.

name: pele-first-world-cup-win
description: A teenage Pele starred as Brazil won the 1958 World Cup in Sweden, announcing one of soccer's greatest players.

name: ali-defeats-liston
description: Cassius Clay, soon known as Muhammad Ali, upset Sonny Liston to win the world heavyweight boxing title.

name: rubiks-cube-invented
description: Hungarian Erno Rubik built a twisting cube of colored squares that became a hugely popular puzzle toy.

name: dungeons-and-dragons-released
description: Gygax and Arneson published Dungeons & Dragons, the first tabletop role-playing game guided by dice and rules.

name: billie-jean-king-battle-sexes
friendly_name: Battle of the Sexes Tennis Match
description: Billie Jean King beat Bobby Riggs in a televised tennis match billed as a contest between the sexes.

name: fischer-spassky-world-chess
description: American Bobby Fischer defeated Boris Spassky in Reykjavik to win the world chess title amid Cold War tension.

name: nadia-comaneci-perfect-ten
description: Gymnast Nadia Comaneci earned the first perfect score of 10 at the Olympics, in Montreal at age fourteen.

name: miracle-on-ice
description: A US college hockey team upset the Soviet squad at the Lake Placid Winter Olympics in a Cold War shock.

name: trivial-pursuit-launched
description: Two Canadians launched Trivial Pursuit, a board game of general-knowledge questions that became a sensation.

name: deep-blue-beats-kasparov
description: IBM's Deep Blue defeated world champion Garry Kasparov, the first computer to beat a top human at chess.

name: tiger-woods-first-masters
description: Tiger Woods won the Masters golf tournament by a record margin, the first Black golfer to take the title.

name: settlers-of-catan-released
description: Klaus Teuber's Settlers of Catan launched in Germany, sparking a global revival of modern board gaming.

name: alphago-beats-lee-sedol
description: DeepMind's AlphaGo defeated Go champion Lee Sedol, mastering a game long thought too complex for computers.

name: usain-bolt-world-record
description: Jamaican sprinter Usain Bolt ran the 100 metres in 9.58 seconds in Berlin, a world record that still stands.

name: wii-motion-gaming
description: Nintendo's Wii used a motion-sensing controller for sports and active games, broadening the gaming audience.
